name: pricedisplay

metadata.name: pricedisplay
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: Terminal display for the Finnish power price.

documentation:
# pricedisplay

Terminal Display for the Finnish Power Price


## Building

You can install the dependencies and build the package with Poetry:

`poetry install`

`poetry build`

You can test the build with:

`poetry run python -m pricedisplay`


## Installing

Install the package via pipx:

`pipx install pricedisplay`

or globally with pip:

`python -m pip install pricedisplay`

Currently you need Python 3.9 or greater.

**NOTE:** On macOS you may get a warning from urllib3. The default installation of Python includes OpenSSL version compiled with LibreSSL, which is no longer supported. You can fix this by installing Python with Homebrew. For details see [this issue](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3020).


## Usage

If you installed with pipx, you can run the display simply with the command:

`pricedisplay`

or if you used pip:

`python -m pricedisplay`

The package creates a settings file config.yml in your config path.

| OS        | Settings Location                             |
|-----------|-----------------------------------------------|
| macOS     | `~/Library/Application Support/pricedisplay/` |
| GNU/Linux | `~/.config/pricedisplay/`                     |
| Windows   | `%APPDATA%\pricedisplay\`                     |

You can also pass your own settings file as an argument:

`pricedisplay --settings PATH`


## Notes

The actual display of the graph depends on your terminal font and colors. For best results you should have UTF-8 encoding and a font which includes unicode East Asian ambiguous characters (in wide format). Some tested combinations are:

| OS        | Terminal         | Font           |           
|-----------|------------------|----------------|
| macOS     | iTerm with Bash  | Hack           |
| GNU/Linux | Gnome with Bash  | Ubuntu Mono    |
| Windows   | Mintty with Bash | Lucida Console | 


## Sample outputs

![sample output minimal](samples/sample_horizontal.png)

![sample output minimal](samples/sample_vertical.png)

![sample output minimal](samples/sample_minimal.png)

modules.name: pricedisplay.application
documentation: -*- coding: UTF-8 -*-

functions.name: pricedisplay.application._InitApp
documentation: Catch errors related to initializing the application.
##  helper functions for reading the settings, initializing and/or resetting the app and running it  ###

functions.name: pricedisplay.application._InitSettings
documentation: Catch errors related to initializing the settings.

functions.name: pricedisplay.application._ResetSettings
documentation: Catch errors related to resetting the settings.

functions.name: pricedisplay.application._ShowErrorMessage
documentation: Show error message or stack trace depending on the debugging level.

functions.name: pricedisplay.application._StartApp
documentation: Catch errors related to starting and running the application.

functions.name: pricedisplay.application.Main
documentation: Parses arguments, reads the config, passes options to the application, and handles any errors.

classes.name: pricedisplay.application.App

methods.name: pricedisplay.application.App._EndCurses
documentation: Reverse terminal settings.

methods.name: pricedisplay.application.App._InitCurses
documentation: Initialize the curses environment.

methods.name: pricedisplay.application.App._InitializeDisplay
documentation: Initialize the price display.

methods.name: pricedisplay.application.App._AvailableFromTime
documentation: Find the datetime after which new price data is expected to be available.

methods.name: pricedisplay.application.App._MidnightUpdate
documentation: Updates the price data and display at midnight.

methods.name: pricedisplay.application.App._DailyDataUpdate
documentation: Checks the data source for new data. If there is price data for tomorrow, updates the display.

methods.name: pricedisplay.application.App._HourlyUpdate
documentation: Updates the display every hour.

methods.name: pricedisplay.application.App._Mainloop
documentation: Mainloop of the application.

methods.name: pricedisplay.application.App.Start
documentation: Initializes curses and display, then runs the Mainloop in the same thread until application is stopped or interrupted.

methods.name: pricedisplay.application.App.Stop
documentation: Stop the application and clear the curses environment.
An application for displaying and updating price data.

modules.name: pricedisplay.datahandler
documentation: -*- coding: UTF-8 -*-

classes.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceData
documentation: Represents the price data and its statistics.

classes.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.DailyData

methods.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.DailyData.Copy
documentation: Copies the data to a new object, so it cannot be replaced.
Represents the price data for yesterday, today, and tomorrow.

classes.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceDataHandler

methods.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceDataHandler._ConvertToPriceList
documentation: Convert a list of ( date, price ) tuples to a list of prices. If data is missing, fill in None.

methods.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceDataHandler._FilterDataByDay
documentation: Filters data based on day to ( date, price ) lists for yesterday, today, and tomorrow.

methods.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceDataHandler._ParseData
documentation: Parses json data to lists of prices on yesterday, today, and tomorrow.

methods.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceDataHandler._ParseObject
documentation: Parses a json object to a datetime object and a two decimal price in cents.

methods.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceDataHandler._ParseDate
documentation: Extracts the date from the json object.

methods.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceDataHandler._ParsePrice
documentation: Extracts the price from the json object and rounds it to two decimals. If there is no specified field, return None instead of a parsing error.

methods.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceDataHandler._RequestDataHTTP
documentation: Requests data with a http get.

methods.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceDataHandler._LoadDataFile
documentation: Reads data from a file.

methods.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceDataHandler._RetrieveData
documentation: Retrieves data either by http request or from a local file.

methods.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceDataHandler.GetPrices
documentation: Returns the prices for yesterday, today, and tomorrow. Makes a deepcopy of the internal data structure to prevent accidental overwriting of the data.

methods.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceDataHandler.MidnightUpdate
documentation: Updates the data at midnight. Moves todays data to yesterday and tomorrows data to today.

methods.name: pricedisplay.datahandler.PriceDataHandler.Update
documentation: Retrieves new data from the source.
Retrieves, parses and updates the price data from the specified source.

documentation: general errors

classes.name: pricedisplay.exceptions.ConfigParsingError

classes.name: pricedisplay.exceptions.DataParsingError

modules.name: pricedisplay.configparser
documentation: -*- coding: UTF-8 -*-

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser._Queries._YesNo
documentation: A simple yes/no prompt.

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser._Queries._OptionQuestion
documentation: A prompt for the user to set an option, with input validation for the option type.

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser._Queries._Validate
documentation: Checks that the given value is of the given type and returns the value in that type. If the type doesn't match, raises a ValueError.

classes.name: pricedisplay.configparser.Config

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser.Config._Edit
documentation: Prompts the user to give a value for each option in the option type. If the option was migrated, skip it. Empty string sets the default.

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser.Config._FindPath
documentation: Finds the path to the config file. Tries first the file supplied as an argument, then the default config path. If no file is found, creates one from template.

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser.Config._FindOldPath
documentation: Finds the path of the last config file before the current version for settings migration.

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser.Config._FindOldVersion
documentation: Checks for old config versions in the config path and returns the newest version.

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser.Config._LoadFile
documentation: Loads the config file as yaml.

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser.Config._Parse
documentation: Parses the config file. Raises ConfigParsingError, if the options in the file have values that don't match with the type defined by the option.

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser.Config._WriteFile
documentation: Writes the config to a file in a neat and readable format.

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser.Config.CreateFromTemplate
documentation: Creates a new config file from a template, and writes it to the default config path. Gives the user an option to edit the values.

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser.Config.Reset
documentation: Asks the user whether to reset the current configuration file, with an option for editing the values. If the user declines, raises a ConfigParsingError.

Represents a configuration loaded from a yaml file. If a path is given, tries it first, then the default user config path. If both fail, creates a new file from a template. The template path can also be given as an argument.

classes.name: pricedisplay.configparser._OptionIterator

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser._OptionIterator._IsOption
documentation: Check if the given item is an option. An option is a dictionary, which has the keys 'value' and 'type'. An option may also contain additional keys.

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser._OptionIterator._MapKeys
documentation: Map keys of nested dictionaries based on a list of indices. Return a list of keys corresponding to those given by the indices.

methods.name: pricedisplay.configparser._OptionIterator._UseKeys
documentation: Uses a list of keys to find the current item in a nested config dictionary.
Iterates through the options in a given yaml representation of a config file.

documentation: -*- coding: UTF-8 -*-

classes.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Point
documentation: Represents a point on the terminal screen.

classes.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Size
documentation: Represents the size of an object.

classes.name: pricedisplay.graphics.BBox
documentation: Represents the bounding box of an object. Two bounding boxes can be added returning a bounding box encompassing them both.

classes.name: pricedisplay.graphics._DisplayWindow

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics._DisplayWindow._FitsInside
documentation: Check that the subwindow fits inside the parent. If no parent was specified, use the whole terminal screen.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics._DisplayWindow.GetBoundingBox
documentation: Returns the bounding box for the display window.
Base class for display windows. Defines a bounding box and checks that the window fits in the parent window.

classes.name: pricedisplay.graphics._PriceDisplayWindow

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics._PriceDisplayWindow._CurrentHourIndex
documentation: Takes into account the daylight saving time change, when finding the index for the current hour.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics._PriceDisplayWindow._PriceToColor
documentation: Finds a curses color pair based on the given price (low, medium, high).
Base class for price display windows. Defines helper functions for color coding prices and taking into account DST changes.

classes.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._AddPadding
documentation: Adds carets to indicate the current hour in the sparklines.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._AddCarets
documentation: Adds carets to indicate the current hour in the sparklines.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._AddLowerCaret
documentation: Add the lower caret to the given lines.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._AddUpperCaret
documentation: Add the upper caret to the given lines.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._AddSymbolAbove
documentation: Finds the highest possible position for the symbol in positive lines, searching from top down.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._AddSymbolBelow
documentation: Finds the lowest possible position for the symbol in negative lines, searching from bottom up.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._AddLowestMarker
documentation: Adds a marker for the lowest price today.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._AddHighestMarker
documentation: Adds a marker for the highest price today.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._AddMissingSymbol
documentation: Adds a symbol for missing prices.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._AddLines
documentation: Adds the sparklines and the lower caret line.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._AddNegativeLines
documentation: Adds negative lines to the graph.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._AddPositiveLines
documentation: Adds positive lines to the graph.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._GetColors
documentation: Gets the colors for the visible price data based on high and low prices.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._GetLimits
documentation: Find the minimum and maximum for the visible prices.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._GetScaledLineParameters
documentation: Find the scaled parameters, when there are both positive and negative prices.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._GetLineParameters
documentation: Find the parameters for drawing the sparklines.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._GetNegativeSparklines
documentation: Get sparklines for negative prices.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._GetPositiveSparklines
documentation: Get sparklines for positive prices.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._GetSparklines
documentation: Gets the sparklines for visible prices.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._GetVisiblePrices
documentation: Gets the prices, which are visible taking into account dst and the number of past hours to show. Returns a list of the visible prices.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph._SplitPrices
documentation: Splits the prices to positive and negative lists, filling in None for missing values.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics.Graph.Update
documentation: Updates the graph, taking into account the changes in dst.

Displays a simple sparkline graph of the power price, color coded based on the limits given in options. The size of the graph, carets used to mark the current hour, and the number of past hours to show can be given as options.
##  display for the price graph  ###

documentation: Adds a detail with a name and price, formatted for the display.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics._DetailWindow._AddMissingDetail

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics._DetailWindow._FormatPrice
documentation: Formats price for display. Ensure two decimal places adding zeros if necessary. Pad with spaces on the left to align decimal point.

methods.name: pricedisplay.graphics._DetailWindow._Normalize
documentation: Normalize the variable to be between limits.
Displays price details in text.
